Background

About This Portfolio

This portfolio documents the hands-on work completed during my IT Specialist internship at Gaudenzia, a nonprofit substance use and co-occurring disorders treatment provider. Over the course of this internship I supported IT operations across multiple facilities in the Baltimore, MD – Washington D.C area, setting up technology infrastructure for staff and ensuring systems ran smoothly.

Founded in 1968, Gaudenzia is one of the largest nonprofit detox and substance use treatment organizations in the northeast United States, serving Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, and the Washington D.C. area — including individuals with co-occurring disorders, HIV/AIDS diagnoses, or criminal justice involvement.

This internship provided practical exposure to enterprise IT tools, hardware, networking, and end-user support in a real-world healthcare environment.

Work Completed

Key Projects & Tasks

New Staff Computer Setup & Onboarding
Ongoing
  • Configured and provisioned computers for incoming staff members across multiple facilities.
  • Installed required software, applied security settings, and connected devices to the network.
  • Set up Brivo access control profiles for new employees — assigning facility groups, configuring credentials, and enabling badge printing.
  • Ensured each workstation and access profile met organizational standards before handoff.
Evidence — Brivo Staff Onboarding
Network Troubleshooting & Infrastructure
Ongoing
  • Diagnosed and resolved network connectivity issues using the Cisco Meraki dashboard.
  • Monitored network health, identified problem devices, and applied fixes to restore service.
  • Worked with APC Back-UPS Pro 1500 uninterruptible power supplies to ensure network uptime.
  • Gained hands-on experience with enterprise-grade cloud-managed networking infrastructure.
Computer Reimaging
Ongoing
  • Reimaged computers to restore clean OS installations for reassignment.
  • Used diskpart in elevated CMD to list, select, and clean drives before imaging.
  • Diagnosed POST errors such as Real-Time Clock Power Loss on HP systems.
  • Ran wpeutil reboot to restart into the imaging environment after disk preparation.
  • Prepared reimaged devices for deployment to new staff or facilities.
Evidence — Reimaging Process
Hardware Upgrades — Memory & Storage Expansion
Ongoing
  • Physically opened and serviced Gaudenzia-tagged HP ProBook 650 G8 laptops and Dell desktops.
  • Installed and upgraded RAM — confirmed 16 GB at 3200 MT/s post-upgrade via Windows System settings.
  • Used precision screwdriver kits to safely disassemble and reassemble devices.
  • After upgrades, ran gpupdate in CMD to refresh Group Policy and ensure domain policies applied correctly with new hardware.
  • Documented hardware changes and verified specs via Windows System Settings.
Evidence — Hardware Upgrades
Disk Space Reclamation — Registry Editing & Profile Cleanup
Desktop Computers
  • Reclaimed disk space on desktops running low due to accumulated old user profiles and temp files.
  • Navigated to C:\Users using cd ..\..\Users in an elevated Command Prompt.
  • Ran rd /s /q <username> to silently and recursively delete old user profile folders.
  • Opened Registry Editor (regedit) as Administrator and navigated to: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList
  • Located and deleted orphaned SID subkeys whose ProfileImagePath pointed to deleted profiles — preventing Windows from trying to load non-existent profiles on login.
  • Cleared leftover temp files from AppData\Local\Temp for additional space recovery.
Evidence — Registry Editing & Disk Cleanup
Printer Installation & Setup — DYMO LabelWriter 450
Multiple Facilities
  • Installed and configured DYMO LabelWriter 450 printers for urinalysis label printing at healthcare facilities.
  • Ran DYMO Connect installer including dependency setup (Microsoft .NET 8 Desktop Runtime).
  • Configured print settings: orientation, label type, and network sharing properties.
  • Used rundll32 printui.dll,PrintUIEntry /ga /n\\<server>\DYMO LabelWriter 450 to deploy printer to all users.
  • Ran rundll32 printui.dll,PrintUIEntry /il to install the printer driver silently.
  • Managed Windows Print Spooler service to resolve printer connectivity issues.
Evidence — Printer Setup
Access Control — Brivo System
Ongoing
  • Managed staff access privileges using the Brivo cloud-based physical access control platform.
  • Created and configured user profiles — assigning groups, setting credentials, and printing access badges.
  • Worked with Brivo door control panels installed across multiple floors and entry points of facilities.
  • Provisioned and revoked access for new and departing employees to maintain building security.
Evidence — Brivo Access Control
Work Phone Setup — Zoom Workspace Business
Ongoing
  • Configured and deployed work phones using Zoom Workspace Business for staff communication.
  • Set up user accounts, extensions, and call settings within the Zoom Phone environment.
  • Provided basic orientation to staff on using the phone system features.
IT Help Desk — Freshservice
Ongoing
  • Logged, tracked, and resolved IT support tickets using the Freshservice ticketing system.
  • Prioritized and responded to requests from staff across multiple facilities.
  • Maintained ticket documentation and updated statuses to reflect resolution progress.
  • In May 2026, supervisor entrusted full independent ticket handling — managing incoming requests, resolving issues, and conducting follow-ups to confirm everything was properly closed.
  • This responsibility demonstrated growth in reliability, communication, and end-to-end IT support ownership.

Looking Back

Reflections & Growth

This internship at Gaudenzia was far more than a technical experience — it shaped me professionally and personally. Working across multiple facilities and supporting a diverse staff taught me the importance of clear communication, patience, and adaptability. I learned to explain IT concepts in simple terms to non-technical colleagues, helping them feel confident with the tools they use every day.

Being trusted to handle the help desk independently in May was a milestone I’m especially proud of. It showed me that reliability and follow-through matter just as much as technical skill. I grew into someone the team could count on.

I also discovered how much I enjoy the human side of IT — being social, approachable, and encouraging to novice users who might feel intimidated by technology. Those moments of teaching someone something new were some of the most rewarding of the internship.

“I am deeply grateful to my supervisors and the entire IT team at Gaudenzia for welcoming me, trusting me with real responsibilities, and investing in my growth. Your patience, mentorship, and encouragement made this experience truly transformative. Thank you.”

Saida Nko’o  ·  IT Specialist Intern  ·  Gaudenzia  ·  March – May 2026
